sections.paragraph:
f. id., Jer 23:40.

כַּלְנֵה Gen 10:10 כַּלִנֶה Amo 6:2 and כַּלְנֹו Isa 10:9, [Calneh, Calno]; pr.n. of a great city subject to the Assyrians; according to the Targums, Eusebius, Jerome, and others, Ctesiphon, situated on the eastern bank of the Tigris, opposite Seleucia. This latter name is said to have been given by Pacorus to this city; see Bochart, Phaleg. iv. 18; Michaëlis, Spicileg. i. p. 228. (The origin of this foreign word does not appear.) [See also כַּנֶּה.]
